model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(total_steps, 1))","old":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, the smallest high-upside change is to improve optimization dynamics rather than add architectural complexity. Switching from Adam to AdamW, raising LR, increasing decoupled weight decay, and making cosine decay respect the actual checker step budget should improve early training progress with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The previous optimizer-focused change did not help, so the next safest high-upside move is to use more examples per update under the same fixed 256-step budget. This MLP is batchnorm-based and currently only sees 16,384 examples total at batch size 64; increasing to 128 should improve gradient quality and sample coverage without adding structural complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest improvement so far came from increasing batch size under the fixed 256-step budget. Pushing from 128 to 256 should let the model cover the full 50k training set within the allotted steps while also giving batchnorm and AdamW lower-variance updates, which is a strong fit for this undertrained flat MLP regime.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last two gains came from improving optimization and sample coverage, so the next logical step is a modest capacity increase. Raising the base width from 192 to 256 hidden units keeps the architecture simple and should let the model use the now-stronger training setup more effectively without a large jump in complexity or runtime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After the recent width increase produced a modest gain, the next simple capacity test is to add one more hidden block while keeping the same optimizer, batch size, and normalization scheme. With the current stronger training setup and fixed-step budget, a third MLP layer is a reasonable way to trade some extra compute for potentially better feature hierarchy without introducing architectural complexity beyond the existing pattern.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent capacity increases have both improved validation loss, so the next efficient probe is another modest width increase while preserving the now-working optimizer, schedule, batch size, and depth. Moving the base width from 256 to 320 hidden units should increase representational power without changing the overall architecture pattern or introducing risky training-loop complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model has become substantially larger while still training for only a short fixed-step budget, so the current AdamW weight decay may now be too strong. Reducing it from 5e-3 to 1e-3 is a simple regularization adjustment that could preserve the optimization gains from the larger batch and deeper/wider network while allowing better fit within the limited training horizon.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous regularization change was effectively neutral-to-worse, so this step restores the better weight decay and tests a higher learning rate on the now larger, deeper model with a large batch. With AdamW, cosine decay, and only 256 optimization steps, the current setup may still be slightly conservative; a moderate LR increase is a strong next probe for better early-budget fit.","secondary_modes":["caching","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The higher learning rate clearly overshot, so this step restores the better 1e-3 setting and continues the more successful capacity-scaling direction. A further width increase is the most coherent next test because batch size, AdamW, cosine decay, and added depth have already improved the optimization regime; the main remaining question is whether the current model is still slightly capacity-limited within the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last width increase was directionally worse, so the best next step is to undo that capacity overshoot and return to the strongest verified width. This keeps the successful deeper architecture and optimized training setup intact while removing the simplest change that appears to have hurt fit under the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With only 256 training steps, cosine annealing may be decaying the learning rate too aggressively for a model that is still capacity-limited and undertrained. Disabling the schedule is a simple way to test whether holding the proven 1e-3 AdamW learning rate constant across the short budget improves final fit without changing the successful architecture or batch regime.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Disabling the schedule was clearly harmful, so this step restores the better cosine decay and tests one more increment in depth, which has been the most consistently positive capacity change so far. A fourth hidden block is a straightforward extension of the current successful MLP pattern and offers more upside than revisiting already weak schedule or learning-rate variants.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"A fourth layer overshot, so this step returns to the better three-block depth and reallocates capacity toward width growth across the existing stack. Increasing the multiplier from 2 to 3 is a cleaner way to test a higher-capacity hidden profile while preserving the training dynamics that worked best so far.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The wider hidden-growth profile was worse, so this step restores the best-known layout and tests a shorter-horizon optimizer tweak instead. Lowering AdamW's second-moment beta can make updates adapt faster within only 256 steps, which is a plausible improvement path now that the simpler architectural scaling directions have started to saturate.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The lower second-moment beta improved short-budget training, which suggests the optimizer benefits from reacting faster. Reducing beta1 modestly is the next coherent refinement: it can make momentum less sticky and help AdamW track the gradient signal more responsively over only 256 steps without changing the now-strong architecture or schedule.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The last improvement came from making AdamW less inertial, so the cleanest follow-up is to continue that trend cautiously. Lowering beta1 again should make first-moment estimates respond even faster within the short 256-step budget, which is a good fit for this fixed-step regime while leaving the rest of the successful setup unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.9)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The optimizer keeps improving as its moving averages become less sluggish, so the next clean refinement is to reduce beta2 as well. With only 256 total steps, a faster second-moment timescale may let AdamW adapt its effective step sizes more quickly while keeping the already successful beta1 setting fixed.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.9)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Lowering beta2 further did not help, so this step restores the better optimizer state and tests a small learning-rate increase on top of the improved AdamW betas. After multiple wins from making the optimizer more responsive, a modest LR bump is a reasonable short-horizon refinement that may extract a bit more progress within the same 256-step budget without changing the architecture.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The small LR increase was slightly worse, so this step restores the better 1e-3 rate and uses the remaining budget to probe regularization around the current best optimizer settings. A modest reduction in AdamW weight decay is a focused test for whether the improved fast-adapting optimizer now wants slightly less shrinkage to get the last bit of short-horizon fit.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The reduced weight decay was effectively neutral, so this final probe restores the slightly better regularization setting and uses the last trial on a modest batch-size increase. Earlier gains came from larger batches under the fixed 256-step budget, and with the now-tuned AdamW betas a moderate move from 256 to 320 is a reasonable final attempt to improve sample coverage and batchnorm stability without a drastic change in training dynamics.","secondary_modes":["caching","indexing"]}
